passages.passage_text: Directions (Q. 91-95): Read the information given below and answer the questions that follow: (i) There are six friends — A, B, C, D, E and F. Together they start a business by investing Rs. 60,000 in total. (ii) A invested twice as much as B while D invested half as much as C. E invested as much as F.

A certain musical scale consist of exactly six notes: F, G, H, I, J and K. The notes are arranged from the lowest note (the first note of the scale) to the highest (the sixth note of the scale). Each note appears once and only once in the scale, and the intervals between the notes are all equal.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following is NOT an acceptable schedule?

choices.a: Two Maths and one Science course
choices.b: Two Science courses and one Maths course
choices.c: Two Fine Arts courses and one Maths course
choices.d: Two Social Studies courses and one Fine Arts course
choices.e: One Social Studies course and two Fine Arts courses

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following courses, when taken with Social Studies, is an acceptable schedule?

choices.a: One course in Maths and one course in Science
choices.b: One course in Fine Arts and one course in English

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the student wishes to take a course in Maths and a course in English, then he must select his third course in the area of

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following pairs of courses CANNOT be combined in an acceptable schedule?

choices.a: A course in Maths and a course in Fine Arts
choices.b: A course in Science and a course in Fine Arts
choices.c: A course in Maths and a course in English
choices.d: A course in Social Studies and a course in Science
choices.e: A course in Science and a course in English

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the student wished to take a course in Science, then which of the following pairs of courses would complete an acceptable schedule?

choices.d: One Science course and one English course
choices.e: One Maths course and one Social Studies course

questions.body: An acceptable schedule CANNOT include two courses in

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If B is not the tallest while F is not the shortest among them, then who invested the most?

choices.a: B is not the tallest while F is not the shortest among them
choices.b: the second tallest person. The man who invested the least stands fourth
choices.c: B invested more than E; D invested less than F
choices.d: the third in order of height

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: What is the rank of F in terms of height?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: What is the rank of B in order of investment?

questions.body: Which of the following CANNOT be true of the scale?
